persons.posture: Standing in a left-facing stance with right foot forward, left leg extended back. Right arm extends forward holding sword horizontally at chest level; left arm bent at elbow, hand near shoulder. Body slightly twisted to the right, weight balanced on front leg. Sword held with both hands, blade pointing forward.
persons.tactic: Mid-level horizontal thrust (中平刺) — a fundamental offensive technique targeting the torso or abdomen.

persons.posture: Facing left, left foot forward, right leg back in a straddle stance. Sword held high above head with both hands, blade angled downward. Torso leans forward slightly, knees bent for stability. Weight distributed evenly between legs.
persons.tactic: High overhead slash (左斜劈) — used to strike downward from above, often to disrupt opponent’s guard or target the head.

persons.posture: In a low horse stance, feet wide apart, knees deeply bent. Sword held horizontally across body at waist height, blade pointing left. Upper body turned slightly right, eyes focused forward. Weight centered over hips and thighs.
persons.tactic: Horizontal sweep (左橫掃) — sweeping motion to clear or unbalance opponent’s lower body or weapon.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg back in a bow stance. Sword held diagonally upward from hip to shoulder, blade angled toward opponent’s upper body. Torso upright, shoulders squared. Weight mostly on front leg.
persons.tactic: Diagonal upward thrust (右斜劈) — targets upper torso or neck area with a rising cut.

persons.posture: Left leg forward, right leg back in a half-step stance. Sword held low and extended forward with one hand, palm facing down. Head turned slightly left, gaze fixed ahead. Weight shifted forward onto front leg.
persons.tactic: Low-level thrust (左平刺) — aimed at groin or thigh, effective in close-quarters combat.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg trailing. Sword held in reverse grip, raised above shoulder with elbow bent. Body twisted sideways, left arm extended for balance. Weight on right leg.
persons.tactic: Overhead reverse cut (右平刺) — defensive or counter-attack move to deflect incoming strikes while delivering a cutting blow.

persons.posture: Feet together, knees bent in a crouched position. Sword held vertically above head, tip pointing up. Arms fully extended upward, palms facing each other. Body upright but leaning slightly backward.
persons.tactic: Vertical defense/counter (左提膝) — preparing to rise into an attack after blocking or evading.

persons.posture: Left leg lifted high in a knee-lift, right leg supporting weight. Sword held horizontally across chest, blade pointing left. Torso upright, arms braced for balance. Eyes focused forward.
persons.tactic: Knee-lift preparatory stance (右提膝) — used to generate momentum for a subsequent kick or strike.

persons.posture: Left leg forward, right leg back in a lunge. Sword held low and extended forward with one hand, blade parallel to ground. Torso leaned forward, hips lowered. Weight concentrated on front leg.
persons.tactic: Forward lunging thrust (左蹬腿) — combines leg extension with sword thrust for maximum reach and force.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg back in a lunge. Sword held high above head with one hand, blade angled downward. Torso twisted slightly right, eyes tracking target. Weight on front leg.
persons.tactic: High descending cut (右蹬腿) — powerful overhead strike intended to break armor or disarm opponent.

persons.posture: Left leg forward, right leg back in a bow stance. Sword held horizontally across body at waist level, blade pointing left. Torso upright, shoulders squared. Weight distributed evenly.
persons.tactic: Horizontal block/thrust (左弓步) — combines defensive posture with immediate offensive capability.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg back in a bow stance. Sword held diagonally upward from hip to shoulder, blade angled toward opponent’s upper body. Torso upright, shoulders squared. Weight mostly on front leg.
persons.tactic: Diagonal upward thrust (右弓步) — targets upper torso or neck area with a rising cut.

persons.posture: Left leg forward, right leg back in a horse stance. Sword held vertically above head, tip pointing up. Arms fully extended upward, palms facing each other. Body upright but leaning slightly backward.
persons.tactic: Vertical defense/counter (左馬步) — prepares for transition into higher or lateral movement.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg back in a horse stance. Sword held horizontally across body at waist level, blade pointing left. Torso upright, shoulders squared. Weight distributed evenly.
persons.tactic: Horizontal block/thrust (右馬步) — maintains balance while ready to pivot or strike laterally.

persons.posture: Left leg forward, right leg trailing in a虚步 (empty step). Sword held low and extended forward with one hand, blade parallel to ground. Torso leaned forward, hips lowered. Weight concentrated on front leg.
persons.tactic: Feinting thrust (左虛步) — deceptive movement to mislead opponent before executing a real attack.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg trailing in a虚步. Sword held high above head with one hand, blade angled downward. Torso twisted slightly right, eyes tracking target. Weight on front leg.
persons.tactic: High descending feint (右虛步) — simulates a powerful overhead strike to provoke defensive reaction.

persons.posture: Left leg forward, right leg back in a standing position. Sword held vertically above head, tip pointing up. Arms fully extended upward, palms facing each other. Body upright but leaning slightly backward.
persons.tactic: Final stance / recovery (左獨立) — returns to neutral position after sequence, ready for next movement.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg back in a standing position. Sword held horizontally across body at waist level, blade pointing left. Torso upright, shoulders squared. Weight distributed evenly.
persons.tactic: Final stance / recovery (右獨立) — completes the sequence with balanced posture, signaling end of form.

persons.posture: Standing in a low horse stance with left leg forward, right leg back; body slightly crouched, weight distributed 60% on front leg; left hand gripping sword hilt at waist level, right hand extended forward holding blade tip upward; eyes focused ahead, head slightly tilted left.
persons.tactic: Basic sword stance for initiating offensive thrust or parry; prepares for follow-up slash or block.

persons.posture: Transitioning to a forward lunge with right leg extended, left knee bent; sword raised diagonally above shoulder, blade angled downward; torso twisted slightly right, gaze fixed on target; weight balanced over front foot.
persons.tactic: Forward thrust technique using momentum of lunge to strike opponent’s midsection or throat.

persons.posture: Backward step into reverse bow stance; sword held horizontally across chest with both hands; body upright, shoulders squared, feet parallel; weight equally distributed between legs.
persons.tactic: Defensive posture to deflect incoming attacks while preparing counter-strike from centerline.

persons.posture: Circular pivot on left foot, right leg trailing; sword sweeping in wide arc from high to low position; arms fully extended, body rotated 90 degrees; head turned to track blade motion.
persons.tactic: Circular slash to clear space or disrupt opponent’s balance; often used after evasion.

persons.posture: Side stance with right leg forward, left leg back; sword held in guard position near face, blade pointing diagonally up; knees bent, hips aligned, weight centered.
persons.tactic: Guard stance ready to intercept or respond to lateral attack; emphasizes quick reaction time.

persons.posture: Jumping leap with both feet off ground, body airborne; sword swung overhead in descending arc toward target below; arms fully extended, core engaged for stability.
persons.tactic: Aerial strike to surprise opponent; targets lower body or legs when enemy is grounded.

persons.posture: Crouching low with knees nearly touching ground; sword held close to body, blade pointed forward along the ground; torso almost parallel to floor, eyes scanning ahead.
persons.tactic: Low sweep or trap technique to disarm or trip opponent’s feet during advance.

persons.posture: Spinning turn on right heel, sword rotating in full circle around body; left arm extended for balance, right arm driving blade outward; dynamic rotational force emphasized.
persons.tactic: Spin-and-slash maneuver to cover multiple angles and disorient attacker.

persons.posture: Standing upright with feet shoulder-width apart; sword held vertically in front of chest, blade facing upward; hands clasped near hilt, elbows tucked inward.
persons.tactic: Stance for mental focus and readiness; may precede any sequence as neutral starting point.

persons.posture: Forward stepping lunge with left foot, right knee bent; sword thrusts straight ahead with full extension of arm; body leans slightly forward, gaze locked on target.
persons.tactic: Direct piercing attack aimed at vital points such as heart or abdomen; requires precision and speed.

persons.posture: Retreating backward step with both feet sliding; sword drawn back in defensive parry, blade angled to deflect incoming blow; shoulders relaxed, hips shifted rearward.
persons.tactic: Evasion combined with defense; sets up counterattack after creating distance.

persons.posture: Kneeling on right knee, left leg extended behind; sword held horizontally across thighs, blade pointing sideways; upper body leaned forward, head lowered.
persons.tactic: Ground-level strike or trapping technique targeting opponent’s legs or weapons during low engagement.

persons.posture: Leaping sideways with sword held diagonally across body; left leg bent, right leg trailing; arms coordinated for momentum control; eyes tracking trajectory.
persons.tactic: Lateral jump strike to bypass frontal defenses and attack flank or rear.

persons.posture: Twisting rotation on left foot, sword swinging in crescent arc from hip to shoulder height; right arm guiding motion, left arm bracing against twist; body torque evident.
persons.tactic: Crescent slash to cut through armor or deflect projectiles; effective in crowded or confined spaces.

persons.posture: Balanced standing pose with sword held low and horizontal, blade near ground; feet planted firmly, knees slightly bent; body angled slightly forward for readiness.
persons.tactic: Low guard for blocking or sweeping; prepares for sudden upward thrust or leg sweep.

persons.posture: Squatting low with sword held vertically above head, blade pointing skyward; both hands gripping hilt tightly; spine straight, hips lowered to ground level.
persons.tactic: Preparation for vertical drop strike or overhead smash; ideal for finishing moves or breaking defenses.

persons.posture: High stance with right leg lifted, left foot planted; sword raised above head in preparatory swing; arms stretched upward, body leaning back slightly for leverage.
persons.tactic: Overhead chopping technique to strike helmeted or armored opponents; relies on gravity and momentum.

persons.posture: Mid-air pivot with sword slicing downward in diagonal line from shoulder to hip; legs crossed in flight, torso twisted for rotational power; eyes following blade path.
persons.tactic: Flying slash to combine aerial mobility with cutting force; targets neck or shoulder area.

persons.posture: Left figure in dynamic forward lunge, right leg extended forward, left leg bent behind for balance. Body angled slightly forward with weight shifted onto front foot. Right arm raised high holding a sword above head, left arm extended backward holding a shield. Eyes focused ahead, suggesting offensive strike or defensive preparation.
persons.tactic: Executing a sweeping downward slash with the sword while advancing with a leap, using the shield for defense and momentum control — a classic martial maneuver combining offense and mobility.

persons.posture: Right figure standing in a stable, grounded stance with feet shoulder-width apart. Slightly crouched with knees bent, weight evenly distributed. Both hands grip a large circular shield held vertically in front of torso, sword drawn back at hip level, ready to parry or counterattack. Head turned slightly to left, observing opponent’s movement.
persons.tactic: Defensive posture with shield as primary protection, preparing to intercept or redirect an incoming attack while maintaining readiness to respond with a counter-strike from the concealed sword.

images.original_text: 起立翻身作滾牌勢以刀循牌一揮進一步為低平勢起立作仙人指路勢

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left foot slightly advanced, body angled slightly to the right. Right hand holds a curved sword extended forward at waist height; left hand grips a round shield held vertically beside the torso. Weight is balanced evenly between both feet, knees slightly bent for readiness.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'Rolling Shield' technique — advancing with a sweeping sword motion while maintaining defensive posture with the shield, preparing for follow-up strikes or transitions.

persons.posture: In a dynamic turning stance, body rotated to face left, right leg trailing behind. Right arm raises the sword high above head in preparation for a downward strike; left arm holds shield in front of chest, angled to deflect or block incoming attacks. Weight shifted onto back leg, ready to pivot or advance.
persons.tactic: Performing a ‘Immortal’s Finger Path’ maneuver — using the shield to control distance while raising the sword for a powerful overhead slash, transitioning from defensive to offensive positioning.

images.original_text: 古拳譜言道：仍以刀循牌一揮作低平勢起立作金雞叫頭勢仍以刀循牌一揮進一步為低平勢起立為躍步勢又以刀循牌一揮進一步為低平勢

persons.posture: Standing in a low, forward-leaning stance with left foot forward and right foot back. Right hand holds a sword raised high above the head, left hand holds a shield in front of the body. Body is angled slightly to the right, weight distributed forward on the front leg.
persons.tactic: Executing a low-level sweeping attack with the sword while maintaining defensive posture with the shield. This represents the 'low flat posture' technique described in the text.

persons.posture: Kneeling or crouching low to the ground with right knee bent and left leg extended backward. Sword held high above the head in a vertical position, shield held in front of the torso. Body is upright but lowered, weight centered over the kneeling knee.
persons.tactic: Performing a rising offensive maneuver from a low position, possibly simulating a counterattack or transition into a leap. The text describes this as 'rising step posture' following the initial low sweep.

persons.posture: 右足在前，左足在後，身體微傾，重心偏於右腳，右手向前伸出呈攻擊或格擋姿勢，左手收於腰際保持平衡與防禦，整體姿態低穩，具備進攻或反擊之準備。
persons.tactic: 此為對敵時的攻防轉換姿勢，可能為『推手』或『封手』類技法，強調重心穩定與手部控制，以應對對方攻勢並尋求反擊時機。

persons.posture: 左足在前，右足在後，身體微傾，重心偏於左腳，左手向前伸出呈攻擊或格擋姿勢，右手收於腰際保持平衡與防禦，整體姿態低穩，具備進攻或反擊之準備。
persons.tactic: 與左者相對，此為對稱之防守或進攻姿勢，可能為『迎手』或『截手』類技法，強調步法靈活與手部節奏，以制敵機先。

images.original_text: 仍作下掃勢左一迴右手仍作一打卽作當頭砲勢左足一打卽作當頭砲勢左手防前右手遮額

persons.posture: Left figure in a dynamic forward lunge with left foot extended, right foot planted back. Right arm is raised high in a sweeping downward motion, left arm extended forward in a blocking or parrying position. Body leans slightly forward with weight distributed on the front leg, suggesting an offensive strike or sweep.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'lower sweep' technique followed by a 'head-on cannon' strike, as described in the text. The movement transitions from a sweeping attack to a direct frontal strike, using the left hand to block while the right hand delivers the main blow.

persons.posture: Right figure in a defensive stance with feet apart, knees bent. Right arm raised above head in a defensive guard, left arm extended forward in a blocking motion. Body is upright but slightly turned, weight balanced between both legs, indicating readiness to counter or evade incoming attacks.
persons.tactic: Defensive posture preparing to intercept or deflect an incoming strike. The text indicates the figure is responding to the opponent’s ‘lower sweep’ by transitioning into a ‘head-on cannon’ strike, suggesting a counterattack initiated after defense.

images.original_text:
卽作五花纏身勢右手右腳右迴
八極拳世譜卷四 拳法譜 十

persons.posture: Standing in a wide, low stance with feet apart and knees bent. Arms are extended outward to the sides, palms facing forward. Body is slightly twisted, weight distributed evenly between both legs. Head turned slightly toward the right, eyes focused forward.
persons.tactic: This posture represents the 'Five Flowers Entwining Body' technique, where the practitioner uses rotational body movement to evade or counter an opponent’s attack while maintaining balance and readiness for offensive action.

persons.posture: Similar stance to Person Left, but mirrored: arms extended outward with palms forward, legs spread wide, knees bent. Body is twisted slightly to the left, weight balanced. Head turned toward the left, gaze steady forward.
persons.tactic: Mirroring the left figure, this position demonstrates the same martial principle — dynamic rotation and body entanglement — used to control space and prepare for simultaneous defense or strike in opposite directions.

images.original_text: 迴身作倒騎龍勢左右手開張作拗單鞭進前仍作埋伏勢一字進坐起立作懸腳虛餌勢仍作下插勢當頭砲勢又作旗鼓勢高四平勢倒插勢卽作一雲步勢拗單鞭勢

persons.posture: Seated on the ground with one leg extended forward and the other bent, arms raised outward in a defensive or preparatory stance. Body is low to the ground, weight distributed on the rear leg, torso slightly twisted for balance. This posture suggests a low guard or transition into a sweeping or trapping technique.
persons.tactic: This position corresponds to the '高四平勢倒插勢' (High Four-Flat Posture, Reverse Insertion Posture), indicating a grounded, stabilizing stance used to counter an opponent’s advance or prepare for a low-level strike or sweep.

persons.posture: Standing in a dynamic, forward-leaning stance with one foot forward and the other back, knees bent, body angled slightly sideways. Arms are extended outward — one hand open and raised, the other bent near the hip — suggesting readiness to strike or block. Weight is shifted forward, indicating momentum or initiation of movement.
persons.tactic: This posture matches '迴身作倒騎龍勢' (Turn body, perform倒騎龍势), which implies a turning maneuver to reposition while maintaining offensive or defensive control. The description suggests a transitional technique combining evasion and counterattack, possibly preparing for a single-strike or hooking motion.

images.original_text:
甲作懸腳虛餌勢左踢右踢驅前進乙作正劉勢左右手遞進作雁翅側身勢跨虎勢相迴立乙卽作懸腳虛餌勢進甲又作立劉勢退兩人卽作雁翅側身勢跨虎勢相迴立

persons.posture: Left figure in a dynamic stance with left leg lifted high in a kicking motion, right leg grounded for balance. The posture suggests an offensive kick combined with defensive hand positioning.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'suspended foot' technique (懸腳) while feigning an attack ('虚餌') to lure the opponent into a vulnerable position, followed by a lateral retreat or counter-strike using the 'goose wing' (雁翅) formation.

persons.posture: Right figure in a reactive stance, stepping back with right leg extended and left leg bent for stability. One arm is raised upward in a blocking or parrying motion, while the other is lowered in a preparatory guard. Body is turned sideways, weight distributed to absorb or deflect incoming attacks, maintaining readiness for counter-movement.
persons.tactic: Responding to the opponent’s suspended foot kick by retreating into a 'cross-legged tiger' (跨虎) stance, then transitioning into a 'hanging foot' (懸腳) counter-attack, utilizing the 'goose wing' (雁翅) body alignment to maintain balance and control during engagement.

images.original_text: 兩人對立作雁翅側身勢 踏虎勢兩手開闔左右相尋

persons.posture: Standing in a side-facing stance with feet apart, left foot forward, right foot back. Arms extended outward to the sides, palms open and facing forward. Body slightly crouched, weight distributed forward on the front leg, torso angled slightly toward the opponent. This is a defensive or preparatory posture for engaging an opponent.
persons.tactic: This posture is described as '踏虎勢' (Treading Tiger Stance), indicating a grounded, powerful stance ready to strike or counter. The arms are open and moving in a circular or alternating pattern ('兩手開闔左右相尋'), suggesting continuous motion to control distance and timing against an opponent.

persons.posture: Facing left, standing in a parallel stance with feet shoulder-width apart. Arms raised at shoulder height, palms facing inward toward the center of the body, fingers spread. Body upright, weight balanced evenly between both legs. This is a neutral or preparatory stance, possibly preparing to engage or respond to the opponent’s movement.
persons.tactic: The posture is labeled '雁翅側身勢' (Goose Wing Side Body Stance), which implies a lateral positioning designed to maintain balance while allowing for quick lateral movement or redirection. The hands are positioned to either block incoming attacks or prepare for a simultaneous strike.

images.original_text:

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-leaning stance with left foot forward, right foot back. Left arm extended upward and outward in a blocking or parrying motion, right arm bent at elbow with hand near chest. Body angled slightly to the right, weight distributed forward on left leg, torso turned toward opponent.
persons.tactic: Executing a '抛架势' (throwing block posture) with the right hand while simultaneously striking the opponent’s right foot back with the left hand, followed by a '拈肘势' (pinch elbow posture) to control or strike the elbow joint.

persons.posture: In a dynamic backward step with right foot trailing, left foot planted firmly. Left arm raised high with open palm facing forward as if deflecting or preparing to strike. Right arm extended backward with fingers splayed, body leaning back for balance. Weight shifted rearward, torso twisted to evade or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Responding to the opponent’s throw-block by retreating and setting up for a counter-strike or defensive maneuver, possibly transitioning into an elbow lock or joint manipulation via the '拈肘势' technique.

persons.posture: Running forward in a low, dynamic stance with left leg extended forward and right leg trailing; arms bent and swinging for momentum; body leaning slightly forward, weight distributed forward to propel movement; head turned slightly toward direction of motion.
persons.tactic: Executing the 'Jia Jin Zuo Fu Hu Shi' (Jia advances into the crouching tiger posture), a martial technique involving forward momentum and low-body control to simulate a tiger’s ambush or leap.

persons.posture: In mid-leap or jump with both feet off ground, knees bent and arms raised upward in a defensive or preparatory grip; torso upright, eyes focused ahead; weight airborne, suggesting an acrobatic or evasive maneuver.
persons.tactic: Performing the 'Yi Zuo Qin Na Shi' (Yi adopts the擒拿势 -擒拿 posture), which involves capturing or controlling an opponent through grappling or joint manipulation, here executed in a dynamic jumping form.

images.original_text: 懶扎衣出門架子變下勢塞步單鞭對敵若無膽向先空自眼瞼手便○金雞獨立顛起裝腿橫拳相兼搶背卧牛雙倒遭著叫苦連天

persons.posture: Standing in a dynamic martial stance with left leg forward and right leg back, body slightly crouched. Weight distributed mostly on front leg, torso angled slightly forward, eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: Executing a defensive or counter-attacking posture known as 'Golden Chicken Standing Alone' (金雞獨立), preparing to strike or block with the raised hand while maintaining balance and readiness for follow-up movement.

persons.posture: In a low, wide stance with feet apart, left leg bent and right leg straight. Right arm extended forward with open hand, left arm raised upward with palm facing outward. Body leans slightly forward, weight balanced between legs, eyes fixed on opponent’s centerline.
persons.tactic: Engaging in a close-range combat technique, possibly initiating a strike or parry. The posture suggests a preparatory phase of the 'Golden Chicken Standing Alone' sequence, aiming to control distance and disrupt opponent’s balance.

persons.posture: Bending forward with right arm wrapped around opponent’s left shoulder, left hand gripping opponent’s back from behind, body angled slightly forward, weight distributed on front foot with rear foot braced for leverage.

persons.posture: Being grabbed by the left shoulder and pulled backward with the right arm encircling the torso; body leaning backward under pressure, feet planted for resistance but unable to counterbalance the force applied from behind.
persons.tactic: Defensive posture being overpowered — this figure is executing a takedown or throw initiated by the opponent’s grip and rotational force. The technique likely involves hip rotation and leg drive to complete the throw.

persons.posture: Standing upright, facing opponent, arms raised in defensive posture, knees bent slightly, weight balanced evenly — appears to be preparing for or reacting to an incoming attack.

persons.posture: In mid-throw position, one leg lifted off ground, torso twisted sideways, arms extended to control opponent’s upper body — dynamic motion indicating completion of a throwing maneuver.
persons.tactic: This depicts the culmination of a joint-locking throw, possibly a variation of ‘flying wheel’ or ‘spinning takedown’ technique common in classical Chinese wrestling and jujitsu styles.

武藝圖譜
左式右式
原戚繼光曰長七尺重三斤八兩刀長二寸有中鋒一面起脊一面有血槽磨重四兩此器法中皆一打一刺而無刃以何為刺今加小刀但刀長則棒頭無力不能壓他棒只可二寸形如鼻嘴打則利棒刺則利刀兩相濟矣南方曰棍北方曰白棒增兵仗記曰棒制有六咸取堅重未為之長四五尺鐵裹其上者謂之詞藜棒昔施銳刀下作倒雙鈎者 棍棒

棍棒譜
原兩人持棍對立作扁身
中攔勢各進一足

persons.posture: Standing in a low, wide stance with feet spread apart; body angled slightly forward, weight distributed evenly; right hand gripping staff near the middle, left hand extended forward holding the staff’s tip; staff held horizontally at waist level, pointing toward opponent.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'middle block' (中攔) technique while advancing one foot to close distance and establish control over the opponent’s weapon line.

persons.posture: In a similar low, wide stance facing opponent; body slightly turned, weight balanced; both hands gripping staff near the center, staff held horizontally at chest height, angled slightly upward to parry or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Maintaining defensive posture while preparing to advance or counterattack, mirroring the opponent’s movement in a symmetrical combat stance.

images.original_text:
仍以棍剪甲棍作大剪勢
武備圖書通志 卷四 根據譜 二十四

persons.posture: Left-facing stance with left foot forward, body slightly crouched. Right hand holds staff extended forward in a horizontal cutting motion, left hand grips staff near the middle for balance. Weight distributed mostly on front leg, back leg slightly bent for stability.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'big scissors' technique with staff — using a wide, sweeping cut to intercept or disarm opponent’s weapon by trapping it between the staff and body.

persons.posture: Right-facing stance with right foot forward, body angled slightly backward. Left hand holds staff extended outward in a defensive or blocking position, right hand grips staff near the end for control. Weight balanced between legs, ready to pivot or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Defensive maneuver to block or deflect incoming strike while preparing to close distance and apply pressure with staff — part of a coordinated pair technique known as 'big scissors'.

images.original_text:

當勢乙又以棍撞甲棍為
大當勢

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left foot forward, body slightly crouched. Right arm extended forward holding a staff horizontally, left arm bent at the elbow with hand near hip. Weight distributed primarily on front leg, back leg providing stability. Staff held parallel to ground, aimed directly at opponent’s midsection.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'Great Guard' (大當勢) technique by raising and striking downward with the staff while simultaneously blocking or deflecting the opponent’s incoming strike.

persons.posture: Facing left, standing in a staggered stance with right foot forward. Both arms engaged: right arm extends staff diagonally upward to intercept incoming blow, left arm drawn back for balance. Body angled slightly sideways to absorb impact and prepare for counterattack. Staff positioned to parry and redirect opponent’s force.
persons.tactic: Responding to an opponent’s ‘Great Guard’ attack by using a defensive block and preparing to transition into a counter-strike or follow-up maneuver, consistent with the text’s description of mutual staff collisions.

persons.tactic: Executing a defensive maneuver by using the staff to deflect an incoming strike while preparing to counterattack with a thrust.

persons.posture: In a dynamic turning stance, right foot pivoting backward, left foot stepping forward. Staff held high above head with both hands, angled downward as if preparing to strike. Body leans forward with weight shifting into the front leg, indicating momentum for an overhead attack.
persons.tactic: Performing a rotational strike (‘滴水勢’ — dripping water posture) to displace opponent’s guard and follow up with a powerful downward blow using the staff.

persons.posture: Left foot forward, right foot back in a straddle stance; body angled slightly left, weight shifted forward onto front leg; right arm extended forward holding staff horizontally, left arm bent at elbow with hand near hip; staff held low, aimed at opponent’s midsection.
persons.tactic: Executing a forward thrust with staff to strike opponent’s chest while advancing one step.

persons.posture: Right foot forward, left foot back in a straddle stance; body upright, weight balanced; left arm extended forward holding staff horizontally, right arm bent with hand near waist; staff held level, positioned to parry or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Defensive posture preparing to block or counter the incoming strike with staff, maintaining distance and readiness for follow-up movement.

persons.posture: Walking forward with left foot leading, body angled slightly right, holding a staff horizontally in both hands at waist level, arms extended, weight shifted forward onto the front leg, preparing to strike downward.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'Drip Water' technique by turning back and using the staff to intercept or control opponent’s weapon, then advancing with a raised staff for a齐眉 (level-forehead) strike.

persons.posture: Retreating backward with right foot trailing, body turned sideways facing left, holding a staff horizontally above head with both hands, bracing against incoming attack, preparing to counter or evade.
persons.tactic: Defensive retreat using the 'Drip Water' stance to create distance, followed by a return step to reengage with a vertical downward strike from raised staff position.

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left foot slightly forward, right foot back; both hands gripping a staff horizontally at chest level, body slightly crouched, weight balanced evenly between feet.
persons.tactic: This is the '大雷' (Great Thunder) technique, indicating a foundational defensive or preparatory posture for staff combat, emphasizing stability and readiness to strike or block.

persons.posture: In a side-stepping lunge with right leg extended forward, left leg bent behind; staff held diagonally across body from upper right to lower left, head turned toward opponent, eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: This is the '天雷' (Heavenly Thunder) technique, suggesting an offensive thrusting motion using the staff to target the opponent’s midsection or head.

persons.posture: Backward step with left foot trailing, right foot planted firmly; staff raised overhead in a vertical guard position, arms fully extended, body leaning slightly backward to maintain balance.
persons.tactic: The '玄雷' (Mysterious Thunder) technique implies a defensive parry or counterattack from above, preparing to deflect incoming strikes while maintaining distance.

persons.posture: Feet shoulder-width apart, knees bent, staff held low near waist with both hands gripping ends; torso twisted slightly to the right, gaze directed forward-left.
persons.tactic: This is the '文雷' (Cultural Thunder) form, likely representing a transitional or preparatory movement for shifting direction or initiating a sweeping strike.

persons.posture: Forward lunge with right leg, left leg trailing; staff angled downward from shoulder height to knee level, hands positioned for rapid follow-up strikes.
persons.tactic: The '樂雷' (Joyful Thunder) technique suggests a fluid, rhythmic attack pattern, possibly combining feints and quick thrusts to disrupt opponent’s timing.

persons.posture: Side stance with left foot forward, right foot flat on ground; staff held horizontally across chest with one hand near center, other hand near end, body angled slightly inward.
persons.tactic: This is the '心雷' (Heart Thunder), indicating a close-range blocking or trapping maneuver designed to control the opponent’s weapon arm or initiate a joint lock.

persons.posture: Wide-legged squatting stance with staff held parallel to ground, extending outward from hip level; arms straight, shoulders relaxed, head upright.
persons.tactic: The '雲雷' (Cloud Thunder) technique represents a wide-area defense or sweeping motion, useful for clearing space or disrupting multiple attackers simultaneously.

persons.posture: Balanced half-kneeling position with right knee on ground, left leg bent; staff held vertically beside head, tip pointing upward, eyes scanning upward.
persons.tactic: This is the '明雷' (Bright Thunder) form, likely used for aerial attacks or sudden rising strikes after evading ground-level threats.

persons.posture: Circular turning motion with both feet pivoting on balls of feet; staff swung in wide arc from left to right, body rotating with momentum, arms extended.
persons.tactic: The '花雷' (Flower Thunder) technique emphasizes rotational power and fluidity, suitable for disorienting opponents or creating openings through circular motion.

persons.posture: Forward advancing step with left foot, right foot trailing; staff held diagonally across body from lower left to upper right, elbows bent for protection.
persons.tactic: This is the '同雷' (Equal Thunder) form, suggesting a coordinated dual-staff or paired-combat technique where timing and synchronization are critical.

persons.posture: Low crouch with knees tucked, staff held horizontally near ground, hands gripping ends close together; body low to avoid high attacks.
persons.tactic: The '光雷' (Luminous Thunder) technique indicates a ground-level sweep or trip, targeting legs or feet to unbalance the opponent during close-quarters engagement.

persons.posture: Standing upright with feet together, staff held vertically above head with both hands; body erect, chin up, eyes fixed forward.
persons.tactic: This is the '土雷' (Earth Thunder) form, representing a static but powerful guard posture, often used as a reset or transition point before launching a major offensive.

persons.posture: Side-step with right foot forward, left foot back; staff held diagonally across body from upper right to lower left, head turned sharply left.
persons.tactic: The '玄雷' (Mysterious Thunder) again — this repetition may indicate variation in application, such as a deceptive feint or directional change during combat flow.

persons.posture: High step with left leg lifted, right leg supporting weight; staff held vertically above head, tip pointed skyward, arms fully extended.
persons.tactic: This is the '上雷' (Upper Thunder) technique, implying an ascending strike or preparation for aerial attack, possibly targeting the head or neck area.

persons.posture: Final stance with feet wide apart, knees deeply bent, staff held low and horizontal across thighs; body lowered into a stable base, hands gripping tightly.
persons.tactic: The '終雷' (Ultimate Thunder) form signifies the culmination of the sequence, often used for finishing moves or grounding oneself after a series of dynamic actions.

persons.posture: Standing in neutral position with feet shoulder-width apart, staff held diagonally across chest with one hand near center, other hand near end; body relaxed but alert.
persons.tactic: This appears to be a transitional or preparatory stance, possibly serving as a reset or bridge between different forms within the system.

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left foot forward, right foot back. Both hands grip the staff horizontally at chest level, arms extended slightly forward. Body is upright, weight balanced evenly between both feet. The staff is held parallel to the ground, ready for thrusting motion.
persons.tactic: This is the 'Advance Staff Thrust' (進棍直刺), a basic offensive technique where the practitioner advances while delivering a straight thrust with the staff to target the opponent’s midsection or throat.

persons.posture: Opposing stance facing Person One, left foot back, right foot forward. Staff held high above head with both hands, angled slightly backward. Body leans slightly backward to prepare for overhead strike or defense against incoming attack.
persons.tactic: This represents the defensive or counter-attack posture in response to the thrust — preparing to block or strike downward with the staff (高舉雙棍).

persons.posture: Left leg bent, right leg extended back in a lunge stance. Staff held diagonally across body from right shoulder to left hip, tip pointing toward opponent’s side. Upper body twisted slightly to generate rotational power.
persons.tactic: This is the 'Shoulder Strike with Staff' (肩擊棍), using body rotation and leverage to deliver a powerful shoulder-mounted strike aimed at the opponent’s ribs or collarbone area.

persons.posture: Feet apart in a wide stance, staff held horizontally across waist level, sweeping from left to right. Arms fully extended, body turned sideways to maximize reach and momentum.
persons.tactic: This depicts the 'Horizontal Sweep' (橫掃棍), used to clear or disrupt an opponent’s lower body or weapon line, often following a missed strike or as a transitional movement.

persons.posture: Right leg forward, left leg back in a half-kneeling stance. Staff raised high above head, angled down toward opponent’s head or neck. Torso leans forward, eyes focused on target.
persons.tactic: This is the 'Diagonal Downward Slash' (斜劈棍), a powerful vertical cutting motion intended to strike the opponent’s skull or upper torso with maximum force.

persons.posture: Facing opposite direction, staff held low near ground, angled upward in preparation for a rising block or counter-slash. Feet in staggered stance for mobility.
persons.tactic: This illustrates a defensive maneuver — possibly preparing to parry or counter the diagonal slash with a rising strike or block.

persons.posture: Both feet together, staff held vertically above head with both hands gripping top end. Body upright, shoulders relaxed, eyes looking up at staff tip.
persons.tactic: This is the 'High Staff Hold' (高舉雙棍), primarily a preparatory or ceremonial stance, but can be used to initiate a descending strike or to guard against overhead attacks.

persons.posture: Slightly crouched, staff held diagonally across body, tip pointing toward opponent’s abdomen. Left hand grips near middle, right hand near tip for control and precision.
persons.tactic: This represents a ‘Diagonal Guard’ or ‘Preemptive Strike’ position, allowing quick transition into either a thrust or sweep depending on opponent’s movement.

persons.posture: Left foot forward, right foot back in a straddle stance. Staff held horizontally across chest, angled slightly downward. Arms bent, elbows close to body for stability.
persons.tactic: This is the ‘Cross-body Block’ (交叉格擋), used to intercept an incoming horizontal strike by redirecting it outward with controlled pressure from the staff.

persons.posture: Feet wide apart, staff held low and extended forward, tip aimed at opponent’s legs. Torso bent forward, knees bent for balance and explosive power.
persons.tactic: This depicts a ‘Low Sweep’ (掃腿式), targeting the opponent’s lower limbs to unbalance them before delivering a follow-up strike or takedown.

此圖為武術招式示範，左側列有縱向格線，右側附有小字註解。圖中無人物或武器，僅為示意結構。

馬上才冠服圖說
凡馬上才著戰笠或著盔衣紅黃號衣紅黃袴不著韝襪 襖與鞋同
戰笠
號衣

馬藝冠服圖說
聲球冠服圖說
增凡擊珠者具襲笠插翠羽虎鬚衣紅貼裏裹肚束廣組帶臂著射韝音鈴也
此圖為武術招式之示意，左右兩側各有一人，呈對峙之勢。左者右足在前，左足在後，身體微傾，右手前伸，左手收於腰間；右者左足在前，右足在後，身體正直，右手握拳上舉，左手平推。雙方皆重心下沉，腳步穩固，以待進攻或防守。圖中無武器，僅以身法與手勢表現技擊之理。

persons.posture: 右足在前，左足在後，身體微傾，右手前伸，左手收於腰間，重心下沉，腳步穩固，呈攻勢準備姿態。
persons.tactic: 此為「迎門三掌」之起手式，以右足為軸轉身發力，右手前推為引敵之勢，左手收腰蓄力待變。

persons.posture: 左足在前，右足在後，身體正直，右手握拳上舉，左手平推，重心穩定，雙目凝視對手，呈防禦兼伺機反擊之姿。
persons.tactic: 此為「封手截腕」之守勢，右手擋頭，左手截臂，以靜制動，待敵出手時即刻反制。
